---
title: "Epilepsy Seizure Counts (Progabide Trial)"
---

Longitudinal data from a clinical trial of the anti-epileptic drug progabide. Each of 59 patients was observed over 4 follow-up periods, with seizure counts recorded at each visit, alongside baseline seizure count and age. Commonly used to teach Poisson regression for count data with repeated measurements on the same subject.


## Source and Citation

- **Citation**: Thall and Vail (1990), distributed in the MASS R package
- **URL**: [https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/MASS/](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/MASS/)
- **License**: Available via the MASS package (part of base R distribution)

## Dataset Characteristics

- **Observations**: 236
- **Variables**: 9
- **Missingness present**: FALSE
- **Longitudinal**: TRUE
- **Hierarchical**: TRUE

## Variable Dictionary

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| TRUE | count | Number of seizures during the follow-up period |
| trt | categorical | Treatment group: placebo or progabide |
| base | count | Baseline seizure count (8 weeks prior to trial) |
| age | continuous | Patient age in years |
| V4 | binary | Indicator for the 4th (final) follow-up period |
| subject | categorical | Patient identifier (1-59), repeated across 4 rows each |
| period | ordinal | Follow-up period number (1 to 4) |
| lbase | continuous | Log-transformed, centered baseline seizure count |
| lage | continuous | Log-transformed, centered age |

## Suggested Learning Objectives

- Fit a Poisson regression model for count outcomes
- Recognize the need to account for repeated measurements per subject
- Compare naive Poisson models to overdispersion-corrected alternatives

## Suggested Classroom Activities

- Model seizure counts as a function of treatment and baseline rate, ignoring then accounting for repeated measures

## Common Student Misconceptions

- Treating each of the 4 visits per patient as an independent observation

## Load This Dataset in R

```r
library(statsdata)
data <- load_dataset("epilepsy-seizures")
```
